assertion_results.text: A SKILL.md file exists in the output skill directory
The SKILL.md has valid YAML frontmatter with name and description fields
assertion_results.evidence: Frontmatter contains: name: pandas-data-cleaning, description: "Guides pandas data cleaning workflows for CSV and Parquet files, including handling missing values, deduplicating rows, normalizing column names, and converting column types...", plus compatibility field

assertion_results.text: The description mentions data cleaning or pandas or csv
assertion_results.evidence: description: "Guides pandas data cleaning workflows for CSV and Parquet files..." — mentions all three: 'pandas', 'data cleaning', and 'CSV'

assertion_results.text: The SKILL.md body covers handling missing values
assertion_results.evidence: Section '### 3. Handle missing values' (lines 77-104) provides a detailed strategy table (Drop rows, Fill with constant, Fill with median, Fill with mean, Forward/back fill, Interpolate) with code examples and guidance on sentinel values

Section '### 4. Deduplicate rows' (lines 107-123) covers key-based deduplication with subset columns, code example using drop_duplicates(subset=key_cols, keep='first'), and explains why reset_index is needed

assertion_results.text: The SKILL.md body covers column name normalization or type conversion
assertion_results.evidence: Section '### 2. Normalize column names' (lines 56-71) covers snake_case normalization with a normalize_columns function. Section '### 5. Convert types' (lines 125-145) covers numeric, date, categorical, and boolean type conversion with errors='coerce'

assertion_results.text: The SKILL.md body contains at least one code example
assertion_results.evidence: Multiple code examples throughout: diagnostic summary (lines 37-49), normalize_columns function (lines 60-71), missing value handling (lines 92-96, 102-104), deduplication (lines 112-119), type conversion (lines 130-141), save operations (lines 149-155), cleaning report (lines 164-177)

assertion_results.text: An evals/evals.json file exists with test cases
assertion_results.evidence: evals/evals.json exists with 3 test cases (ids: 1, 2, 3) covering CSV cleaning with dedup, messy column names with mixed dates, and parquet file with mixed types
